body.title: [triage] Scenario generator: auto-generate test scenarios from intent + constraints
body.intention: I intend that test scenarios are compiled from intent + normalized constraints rather than written by hand.
body.description:
The completion criteria field on tickets is manually-written scenarios in prose. A scenario generator takes (extracted intent, normalized constraints) and produces structured test scenarios — the compiled-inference version of the test plan.

This is the core capability of the automated test builder observed at a parallel project (specifically designed so non-automation engineers can create automated cases from intent descriptions). Our factory will eventually produce a general-purpose version of this.

Pipeline position: intent-extractor → constraint-normalizer → scenario-generator → judge.

Preconditions: T-intent-extractor-agent and T-constraint-normalizer-agent should be solid before this is ready to sprint.

**Affected files:** TBD — new capability; depends on intent extractor + normalizer outputs
**Design rules:** none apply at design stage
**Scope boundary:** IN — scenario list from (intent, constraints). OUT — executing scenarios (that's the test runner) or judging results (that's judge-agent).
**Completion criteria:** Scenario generator produces ≥3 distinct test scenarios from a sample ticket intent; at least 2 are non-obvious (not just the happy path).
